figure.title: Vendor Defined Packet TLV mapping to UART Stream Transport Messages
figure.purpose: Illustrates how a single Vendor Defined Packet spans across multiple UART stream transport messages.

technical_summary: A Vendor Defined Packet (Type-Length-Value or TLV) can be larger than a single UART stream transport message.
The diagram shows one such packet spanning three distinct transport messages (messages #1, #2, and #3).
Each transport message has its own length field (L=3, L=2, L=3).
The Type and Length (TL) DWord and Value DWords (V0, V1, V2, etc.) of the payload are distributed across these messages.

packet_layout.description: Transport container with its own Length field (L) and payload.
Payload consisting of TL (Type-Length) and V (Value) DWords distributed across multiple transport messages.

rules: There is no fixed relationship between the UART Stream transport Message length and the Vendor Defined Packet length; packets can span multiple messages.

critical_facts: The UART sideband can transport larger encapsulated packets by fragmenting them into multiple UART transport messages.
